title: cota tinctoria var. discoidea
intro: Cota tinctoria var. discoidea, commonly known as dyer's chamomile or wild chamomile, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to Europe. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating various health issues such as digestive problems, skin irritations, and menstrual cramps. The health benefits of Cota tinctoria var. discoidea include reducing stress and anxiety, promoting relaxation, and improving sleep quality. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for its medicinal use.
